findings.description: The North Carolina state seal image at the top of the letter is missing in the HTML rendering
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the North Carolina state seal/logo at the top center. HTML page 2 (the corresponding content page) does not display this seal image.

findings.description: The HTML conversion renders the entire multi-page document as a continuous flow with embedded images of the original PDF pages alongside the text conversion, creating a confusing dual-rendering
findings.evidence: Pages 2-4 in the HTML show both an embedded image of the original PDF letter AND a text-based HTML conversion of the same content below it, effectively duplicating content

findings.description: The email chain reading order is confusing due to embedded image versions of the same content appearing alongside text versions
findings.evidence: The HTML rendering shows embedded screenshots/images of the PDF pages interspersed with the text conversion of the same content, creating a duplicated reading experience throughout pages 5-16

findings.description: The DEQ logo at the bottom of the letter page appears but is less clear in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a clear DEQ logo. The HTML rendering includes the logo but at a different scale.

findings.description: The signature of David Miller is missing in the HTML text conversion
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows David Miller's handwritten signature. The HTML text version on page 4 only shows 'for David Miller' and 'State Mining Engineer' without the signature image.

findings.description: Colonial Pipeline Company logo/icons in email signatures vary in rendering quality
findings.evidence: PDF shows Colonial Pipeline social media icons and logo clearly; HTML renders them but with some variation in presentation

findings.description: Underlined section headings in Attachment 1 lose their distinctive formatting emphasis in some HTML renderings
findings.evidence: PDF pages for Attachment 1 show clearly underlined section headings like 'Notice of Blasting Related Activities', 'Information to be Provided in Blasting Plans', etc. The HTML text rendering preserves bold/underline in some places but the visual distinction is less clear.

findings.description: Mark Piazza's signature on the Colonial Pipeline letter is missing in the HTML text conversion
findings.evidence: PDF page 17 shows Mark Piazza's handwritten signature. The HTML text version does not include this signature image.

findings.description: Rep. Dennis Riddell's email signature block with NC state seal is reformatted and the seal image positioning differs
findings.evidence: PDF shows the NC state seal as part of Rep. Riddell's email signature block in a specific layout. HTML renders it differently with the seal image separated from the signature text.

findings.description: Multiple h1 elements used throughout the document. The first h1 is 'Colonial Pipeline Company' and there are additional h1-level headings. The document has inconsistent heading hierarchy with h2 and h3 mixed without clear structure.
findings.evidence: HTML contains h1 for 'Colonial Pipeline Company' and also uses h1:first-of-type styling. Multiple h2 and h3 elements are used for section headings like 'ATTACHMENT 1', 'BLASTING REQUIREMENTS AND TECHNICAL CONDITIONS', 'QUARRY OPERATION REQUIREMENTS AND TECHNICAL CONDITIONS' etc.

findings.description: The 'Wehner, Judy' email header is rendered as an h2 heading, but in the PDF it is simply a name/label at the top of an email printout, not a document section heading.
findings.evidence:
HTML: '

Wehner, Judy

' — PDF shows 'Wehner, Judy' as a header field of the email, not a section heading.

findings.description: The formal letter on page 1 references 'GS 74-50 and 51' but the HTML renders it the same way. However, minor formatting differences exist in how the letter body is structured with blockquote vs. original indentation.
findings.evidence: This is acceptable reformatting, not a content error.

findings.description: The HTML renders Rep. Dennis Riddell's name in a cursive font style which is a stylistic interpretation, but the text content 'Dennis' matches the PDF's signature block.
findings.evidence:
HTML: '

Dennis

' — PDF shows 'Dennis' in a stylized/signature font.

findings.description: Several images have empty or generic alt text that could be more descriptive.
findings.evidence: Multiple instances of alt='' for images on pages 5, 6, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 where they appear to be decorative spacers or logos. The signature image alt text 'Signature of David Miller' is reasonable.

findings.description: The HTML includes multiple nested <main> elements which is structurally unusual but does not duplicate content. Each page's content appears once.
findings.evidence: HTML has approximately 12+ <main> elements nested or sequential, representing different pages.

findings.description: No significant sections are missing. All pages from the PDF (1-17) appear represented in the HTML, including the formal letter, email chain, Colonial Pipeline cover letter, Attachment 1, and Exhibits.
findings.evidence: All major sections present: formal DEQ letter, email exchanges, Colonial Pipeline supplemental comments letter, Attachment 1 blasting requirements (Sections A-M), Quarry Operation Requirements (Sections A-B), and Exhibits 1A/1B references.
